steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: A participial phrase "Having 9,721 feet of prominence," is followed by a blank space that must be filled with an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the dangling modifier rule
steps.content:
A participial phrase must be immediately followed by the noun it logically modifies — the thing that actually "has" the prominence.

A: "Having 9,721 feet of prominence, Pico do Ramelau..." — Pico do Ramelau is the mountain with that prominence. Modifier is correctly attached. Correct.

B: "Having 9,721 feet of prominence, the list of mountains..." — A list cannot have topographical prominence. Dangling modifier.

C: "Having 9,721 feet of prominence, geographers..." — Geographers don't have 9,721 feet of prominence. Dangling modifier.

D: "Having 9,721 feet of prominence, the ranking..." — A ranking cannot possess prominence. Dangling modifier.
